task_completion.description: Log into OpenClinica and access the CV-REG-2023 study.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenClinica web interface logged in (e.g., as root) with the active study set to or showing 'CV-REG-2023'.

task_completion.description: Open the provided MedDRA coding dictionary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the file '/home/ga/meddra_extract.csv' opened in a text editor, spreadsheet viewer, or its contents displayed in a terminal.

task_completion.description: Update the discrepancy note for subject CV-101 with the correct MedDRA code and change its status from 'New'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenClinica discrepancy note update form for subject CV-101. The resolution text box contains the code '10028813' and the status dropdown is set to a value other than 'New' (e.g., 'Updated', 'Resolved', 'Closed').

task_completion.description: Update the discrepancy note for subject CV-102 with the correct MedDRA code and change its status from 'New'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenClinica discrepancy note update form for subject CV-102. The resolution text box contains the code '10019211' and the status dropdown is set to a value other than 'New'.

task_completion.description: Update the discrepancy note for subject CV-103 with the correct MedDRA code and change its status from 'New'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenClinica discrepancy note update form for subject CV-103. The resolution text box contains the code '10013573' and the status dropdown is set to a value other than 'New'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenClinica web interface to resolve the notes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the OpenClinica UI and filling out web forms, rather than using terminal commands (like curl or psql) to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened and viewed the dictionary file to find the codes.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be at least one screenshot showing the contents of 'meddra_extract.csv' being viewed, proving the agent didn't use prior knowledge or hallucinate the expected codes.

integrity.description: The agent manually entered the resolution text and updated the status in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing or pasting the codes into the actual OpenClinica text fields and interacting with the status dropdown menus.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected MedDRA LLT numeric codes to be entered in the discrepancy note resolutions are: 10028813 for subject CV-101, 10019211 for subject CV-102, and 10013573 for subject CV-103. The study identifier is CV-REG-2023 and the dictionary file is located at /home/ga/meddra_extract.csv.
